functions: Homologous recombination repair (HRR) regulation via double-strand break resection
DNA damage response signaling
Cell cycle checkpoint activation
Protein ubiquitination
Transcriptional regulation
Chromatin remodeling and epigenetic modification
Maintenance of genome integrity
DNA replication fidelity during early embryogenesis
pubmed.druggability_notes: BRCA1-deficient (homologous recombination-deficient) tumors are sensitive to PARP inhibitors (e.g., olaparib, niraparib, fluzoparib) via synthetic lethality. Structural/functional domains include the RING domain and coiled-coil region (PALB2 interaction). PARP inhibitor resistance arises from secondary intragenic mutations restoring partial BRCA1 protein function. In BRCA1/2-WT ccRCC, SLFN11 upregulation boosts proteasomal degradation of BRCA1, creating acquired PARP-1 dependency that sensitizes tumors to alkylating agent + PARP inhibitor combination (TMZ + olaparib).
cellular_states: Early embryonic cells during maternal-to-zygotic transition
Germ layer-forming cells during early development

functions: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase mediating 'Lys-6'-linked polyubiquitin chain formation
Central role in DNA repair and cellular response to DNA damage
Component of BRCA1-BARD1 heterodimer coordinating DNA repair, ubiquitination and transcriptional regulation to maintain genomic stability
Regulates centrosomal microtubule nucleation
Required for S-phase and G2 cell cycle arrest after ionizing irradiation
Contributes to homologous recombination repair via interaction with PALB2 and loading of BRCA2-RAD51 machinery
Inhibits lipid synthesis by binding inactive phosphorylated ACACA and preventing dephosphorylation
Acts as a transcriptional activator
cellular_states: Cells responding to ionizing radiation / DNA damage
uniprot.druggability_notes: No explicit druggability or binding pocket mentions in the provided text. BRCA1 is an E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase (transferase) with a zinc-finger / RING domain (zinc ion binding); loss-of-function mutations in BRCA1 underlie sensitivity to PARP inhibitors via synthetic lethality, though this is not stated in the text.

functions: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase mediating 'Lys-6'-linked polyubiquitin chain formation
Central role in DNA double-strand break repair and DNA damage response
Forms BRCA1-BARD1 heterodimer coordinating DNA repair, ubiquitination and transcriptional regulation to maintain genomic stability
Contributes to homologous recombination repair via direct interaction with PALB2, modulating PALB2-dependent loading of BRCA2-RAD51 machinery at DNA breaks
Required for S-phase and G2/M cell cycle arrest after ionizing radiation; regulates CHEK1 activation via the BRCA1-RBBP8 complex
Required for FANCD2 targeting to sites of DNA damage
Regulates centrosomal microtubule nucleation
Inhibits lipid synthesis by binding inactive phosphorylated ACACA and preventing its dephosphorylation; acts as a transcriptional activator
